LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS VICTORIA NAVARRETE AND FERNANDO NAVARRETE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OR BAR THE USE OF SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE IS GRANTED. WITHIN 5 DAYS (BY OCTOBER 29, 2013 BY HAND DELIVERY), DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE THE SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE. THIS EVIDENCE IS RELEVANT AND DIRECTLY RESPONSIVE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES. (CCP 2024.050(B)(1).) PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY MET AND CONFERRED PRIOR TO FILING THE INSTANT MOTION. (CCP 2024.050(B)(2).) PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE FOR NOT BRINGING THIS MOTION EARLIER BASED ON THE DISCLOSURE OF THIS EVIDENCE FIVE DAYS PRIOR TO THE INITIAL TRIAL DATE. (IBID.) DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT THE SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE WILL ONLY BE USED AS IMPEACHMENT EVIDENCE IS NOT A BAR TO PRODUCTION OF RELEVANT EVIDENCE PURSUANT TO PROPERLY SERVED FORM INTERROGATORIES. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTION THAT THE SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE IS ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT IS DISINGENUOUS. DEFENDANT CANNOT CLAIM WORK PRODUCT TO AVOID DISCLOSURE OF THE RELEVANT SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE AND ALSO INTEND TO INTRODUCE IT FOR IMPEACHMENT PURPOSES AT TRIAL. SUCH A CLAIM WOULD PRECLUDE ITS INTRODUCTION OF THE EVIDENCE AT TRIAL. COUNSEL STIPULATE TO ALLOW PLAINTIFFS MORE TIME TO FILE A RELATED MOTION IN LIMINE, SHOULD IT BECOME NECESSARY. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: JULIEANN HAMILL, CSR #5151 OF NOELLE OTTOBONI & ASSOCIATES (650.588.7404) (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS VICTORIA NAVARRETE AND FERNANDO NAVARRETE'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY FOR SOLE PURPOSE TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OR BAR THE USE OF SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE.

Appearances: E. Gerard Mannion of Mannion & Lowe (415.733.1052) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Deborah Bjonerud of Philip M. Andersen & Associates (925.225.6838) for defendants and responding parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted for the sole purpose of allowing Plaintiffs to make a motion compel production or bar the use of Defendants' surveillance. This evidence is relevant and directly responsive to form interrogatories. (CCP 2024.050(b)(1).) Plaintiffs have sufficiently met and conferred prior to filing the instant motion. (CCP 2024.050(b)(2).) Plaintiffs have demonstrated good cause for not bringing this motion earlier based on the disclosure of this evidence five days prior to the initial trial date. (Ibid.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Julieann Hamill, CSR #5151 of Noelle Ottoboni & Associates (650.588.7404) (302/EHG)

Mtn To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence; Compendium
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS VICTORIA NAVARRETE AND FERNANDO NAVARRETE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OR BAR THE USE OF SURVEILLANCE EVIDENCE.

Appearances: E. Gerard Mannion of Mannion & Lowe (415.733.1052) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Deborah Bjonerud of Philip M. Andersen & Associates (925.225.6838) for defendants and responding parties.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: Granted. Within 5 days (by October 29, 2013 by hand delivery), Defendant shall produce the surveillance evidence. This evidence is relevant and directly responsive to form interrogatories. (CCP 2024.050(b)(1).) Plaintiffs have sufficiently met and conferred prior to filing the instant motion. (CCP 2024.050(b)(2).) Plaintiffs have demonstrated good cause for not bringing this motion earlier based on the disclosure of this evidence five days prior to the initial trial date. (Ibid.) Defendant's argument that the surveillance evidence will only be used as impeachment evidence is not a bar to production of relevant evidence pursuant to properly served Form Interrogatories. Defendants' objection that the surveillance evidence is attorney work product is disingenuous. Defendant cannot claim work product to avoid disclosure of the relevant surveillance evidence and also intend to introduce it for impeachment purposes at trial. Such a claim would preclude its introduction of the evidence at trial. Counsel stipulate to allow plaintiffs more time to file a related Motion in Limine, should it become necessary. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Julieann Hamill, CSR #5151 of Noelle Ottoboni & Associates (650.588.7404) (302/EHG)

Mtn To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence; Compendium

Deborah T. Bjonerud, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates [925.225.6838] appearing by telephone for Defendants ERIC CHUN KHUN LEE and LUN WAH LEE
